byRonald Clark

EUGENE, Ore —It's just days away from the opening ceremony of the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, and one of the main events expected to garner attention is women's gymnastics.

Over the last couple of years, the sport has become a main spectacle on the docket of events and this year's USA roster is expected to compete for gold once again.

The squad includes one new comer in Hezly Rivera, but returns four stars from the previous team: Suni Lee, Jordan Chiles, Simone Biles, and Oregon state's own Jade Carey.

See our full interview with her here:

Jade Carey - Full Interview

When speaking with the media on Wednesday, Carey said she's excited to compete in her second Olympics and actually experience the fanfare. During the Tokyo games, covid restrictions were still in place and limited the access of the athletes. This year the gold medalist is looking forward to competing in front of a full crowd including her family and friends.

Carey said she can't wait once again put on the red, white, and blue and not only represent her country but Beaver Nation as well.

"It means a lot to me to be able to represent Oregon State in Paris on such a world stage," she said. "This school has done so much for me and has gone above and beyond to get me to where I am today. So just being able to represent them and give them my best foot forward every day."

The qualifying round for women gymnastics begins on Sunday, July 28th.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
